title: Global Monitoring — Push Template
short_description: Pub/sub push template: hourly cron reads this tenant's own status and pushes it to the global-monitoring dashboard. Self-serve — required config is just a read token, the tenant's API URL, and a label; what-to-share (tier) and cadence are optional.
release_notes: v0.7.0: tenant-configurable what + cadence. monitoring_tier (T0-T3, default T3) projects each window before sending, so the tenant controls how much detail leaves. monitoring_push_interval_minutes (default 60) gates push frequency, persisted in the new monitoring_push_state table (kv is session-scoped). Both optional and default in code, so self-serve install is unchanged.

required_secrets.name: SERVICE_MONITORING_TOKEN
required_secrets.description: Bearer token for reading this same tenant's own communications/issues/alerts before pushing the summary out. Sent as Authorization: Bearer. Legacy API Key values are still tolerated by the code.

required_global_variables.name: self_api_url
required_global_variables.display_name: This tenant's own API base URL
required_global_variables.description: This tenant's own API base URL, used for the self-read. Pattern: https://<tenant>.api.<env>.wonderful.ai (e.g. https://eventim.api.wonderful.ai).

required_global_variables.name: tenant_label
required_global_variables.description: Display name this tenant is shown as on the dashboard. Not hardcoded in code so this same plugin can be installed on multiple tenants.

required_global_variables.name: monitoring_tier
required_global_variables.description: How much detail leaves this tenant: T0 (name+severity), T1 (+health rollup), T2 (+per-agent KPIs, business metrics, monitor rollups), T3 (+full alert/monitor lists). Optional — defaults to T3 (full).

required_global_variables.name: monitoring_push_interval_minutes
required_global_variables.description: Minimum minutes between pushes. The cron fires hourly; a run is skipped if the last push was more recent than this. Optional — defaults to 60 (push every run). Set e.g. 360 to push every 6h.
